detail.title: New CLI Subcommands — preflight / lint / audit

produces.artifact: arc eval lint terminal output (validation errors + warnings)
arc eval audit terminal output (assertion promotion and retirement candidate lists)

requirement.title: arc eval preflight Subcommand
requirement.description: A new arc eval preflight <scenario> subcommand MUST run 2–3 baseline pilot trials against the scenario (no skill, no treatment), compute pass rate, and record the result keyed by scenario hash. Outcomes: PASS when baseline pass rate is below the ceiling threshold (0.8 by default); BLOCK when the ceiling is reached, signaling the scenario is not discriminative. Subsequent arc eval ab runs against the same scenario hash MUST require a prior PASS preflight record; missing or stale records MUST block ab with a clear remediation message. Scenario hash is computed deterministically from the scenario file contents, so unchanged scenarios skip preflight transparently. Preflight is exempt from the k≥5 verdict policy (fr-vr-001) because it checks scenario validity, not shipping fitness — its output is PASS/BLOCK, never SHIP/INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a scenario that has never been preflighted, arc eval ab MUST refuse to run and MUST print a message instructing the user to run arc eval preflight first. fr-cli-001

Given a scenario whose baseline pass rate across 2–3 pilot trials is ≥ 0.8, arc eval preflight MUST output BLOCK with a clear explanation (baseline ceiling reached) and MUST persist the block record keyed by scenario hash. fr-cli-001
Given a previously preflighted scenario whose file contents are unchanged (identical hash), arc eval ab MUST run without re-requiring preflight. fr-cli-001
Given a scenario edited after a prior preflight, the scenario hash MUST differ and arc eval ab MUST refuse until preflight is re-run. fr-cli-001
arc eval preflight MUST NOT emit SHIP, NEEDS_WORK, BLOCKED, or INSUFFICIENT_DATA verdicts. Its only outputs are PASS and BLOCK. fr-cli-001

requirement.title: arc eval lint Subcommand
requirement.description: A new arc eval lint <scenario> subcommand MUST validate a scenario file against a JSON Schema covering required sections (Context, Setup, Grader Config, Assertions), section format, assertion shape, and grader config shape. Errors MUST be reported with file location and remediation. Lint MUST exit non-zero on any schema violation.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a scenario with a well-formed structure, arc eval lint MUST exit 0 and print "ok". fr-cli-002
Given a scenario missing a required section such as "## Assertions", arc eval lint MUST exit non-zero and print a diagnostic identifying the missing section and the expected location. fr-cli-002
Given a scenario with an assertion entry that lacks a grader-type field, arc eval lint MUST exit non-zero and report the specific assertion id. fr-cli-002

requirement.title: arc eval audit Subcommand
requirement.description: A new arc eval audit subcommand MUST aggregate discovered_claims[] and weak_assertions[] across the entire benchmark history and emit two lists: "promotion candidates" (claims that recur frequently and fail often — candidates for elevation to formal assertions) and "retirement candidates" (assertions repeatedly flagged as weak or non-discriminative). Output MUST include frequency counts, originating scenarios, and a stable hash so the same audit run is reproducible.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a benchmark history containing at least 10 graded trials across multiple scenarios, arc eval audit MUST emit at least one promotion candidate or one retirement candidate, each with frequency data. fr-cli-003
The promotion-candidate list MUST sort claims by descending frequency of occurrence multiplied by failure rate, so high-impact candidates surface first. fr-cli-003

arc eval audit MUST be read-only relative to scenarios and assertions — it MUST NOT modify evals/scenarios/*.md files. Promotion and retirement are human-arbitrated actions performed manually after reviewing the audit. fr-cli-003
